festivals.name: Indra Jatra
festivals.description: Kathmandu's greatest festival celebrates the rain god Indra with the living goddess Kumari's chariot procession.

festivals.name: Tihar (Festival of Lights)
festivals.description: Five days of lights, colours, and animal worship culminating in Lakshmi Puja with oil lamps and fireworks.

destinations.name: Kathmandu Valley
destinations.description: Seven UNESCO monument zones including Pashupatinath, Boudhanath, and three Durbar Squares.

destinations.name: Pokhara
destinations.description: Nepal's adventure capital beside Phewa Lake, with Annapurna views and world-class paragliding.

destinations.name: Chitwan National Park
destinations.description: UNESCO Terai park with one-horned rhino, Bengal tigers, and over 500 bird species.

destinations.name: Annapurna Base Camp
destinations.description: The classic trek to a natural amphitheater at 4,130m surrounded by ten peaks over 7,000m.

destinations.name: Langtang Valley
destinations.description: Nepal's closest major trekking destination to Kathmandu with stunning glacial landscapes and Tamang villages.

destinations.name: Upper Mustang
destinations.description: A remote rain-shadow kingdom of cave cities and monasteries carved into cliffs; requires special permit.

destinations.name: Gosaikunda Lake
destinations.description: A sacred high-altitude lake at 4,380m created by Shiva's trident, revered Hindu pilgrimage site.

destinations.name: Bardiya National Park
destinations.description: Nepal's largest park in the western Terai, less visited than Chitwan but with excellent tiger sightings.
